name: Jatayu

distinctive_features: Regal bearing indicative of a king
visual_attributes.texture: Feathery with a slightly rugged appearance
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Large, comparable to a giant vulture
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Wings spread wide, poised for flight or battle

domain_specific_visual.AGE_INDICATOR: Signs of age and wisdom in features

domain_specific_functional.SACRIFICIAL_ACT: Attempted to save Sita from Ravana
domain_specific_functional.GUIDANCE_ROLE: Informed Rama and Lakshmana about Sita's abduction

relationships.description: Jatayu's elder brother who sacrificed his wings to save Jatayu

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: India, specifically Kerala and Andhra Pradesh
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Symbol of bravery, sacrifice, and loyalty
primary_sources: Local lore of Kerala and Andhra Pradesh

contrastive_constraints: NOT a young or small bird
NOT devoid of mythological or divine attributes
